nodes.title: Use an async function as the entry point

anchor.snippet: export default async function addTwoPromises(promise1, promise2) {

nodes.why: `async` lets us use `await` directly, so the flow reads top-to-bottom.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner to read, while still returning a Promise to the caller.

nodes.title: Wait for both promises concurrently
nodes.why: `Promise.all` starts both operations and waits once, which is faster than awaiting one-by-one.
nodes.alternative: Await `promise1` and `promise2` in two separate lines.
nodes.tradeoff: Better latency and simple code, but it fails fast if either promise rejects.

nodes.title: Return the resolved sum
nodes.why: Once both numbers are resolved, the function returns the exact combined result.
nodes.tradeoff: Direct and readable, assuming both promises resolve to numbers as expected.
